mappings.rationale: Covers cell membrane structure, compartmentalization, osmosis, and active transport—key topics in the molecular-level vital processes outlined here.
Directly addresses the nerve impulse, neuron structure, synaptic transmission, and bioelectricity in neural systems.
Covers the biochemical basis of membrane transport, ion channels, and the molecular mechanisms of nerve impulse transmission.
Addresses molecular mechanisms underlying cell membrane function, signal transduction, and ion transport processes.
Covers neural signal transmission, synapse function, and the neuroscience underlying nerve impulse processing.
Addresses muscle contraction (striated, cardiac, and smooth muscle) and nerve impulse transmission as core physiological processes.
Covers the molecular basis of membrane structure, ion channels, and the chemistry underlying bioelectricity and bioluminescence.
